name: Sun Jian (Wentai)
page_title: Sun Jian (Wentai) 孫堅 (文台) [Han, Yuan Shu, Wu] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
ranks: General Who Routs the Caitiffs, Inspector of Yu Province
novel_notes:
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
	
		
	Sun Jian gave the Imperial Seal to Yuan Shu after finding it in a well. He did not hold the Seal for himself.
	During the coalition against Dong Zhuo, Sun Jian served as a subordinate of Yuan Shu, not as himself.
	Sun Jian did not withdraw from the anti-Dong Zhuo alliance, but rather was the only general to achieve success in the operations.
	Sun Jian attacked Liu Biao on Yuan Shu’s orders, not because Liu Biao attacked first. In addition, during this time, Sun Jian was based in Henan, not Changsha as the novel states.
	Sun Jian never killed the fictional Chen Sheng.
	The ‘Magnificent Five of Wu’ is a fan creation with no historical basis. Sun Jian was not a part of any such group.

sections.biography-internet-trusted:
Sun Jian gained fame in his youth for a daring attack on a group of pirates and then for defeating the rebel Xu Chang. Made Zhu Jun’s Associate Major during the Yellow Turban rebellion, gathering a thousand men and matchless in deed, he was first over the walls at Wancheng.

Sun Jian fought against Bian Zhang in Liang under Zhang Wen and defeated Ou Xing and his allies, for which Wentai was enoffed as Marquis of Wucheng. Sun Jian raised troops against Dong Zhuo, executing Wang Rui and Zhang Zi along the way before meeting up with Yuan Shu. Sun Jian met with great success against the Imperial Army, killing Hua Xiong in the process and was the first into Luo Yang.

Sun Jian was sent against Liu Biao by Yuan Shu where he defeated Huang Zu but was soon killed by an arrow in uncertain circumstances.

full_text:
Sun Jian (Wentai) 孫堅 (文台)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Sun Jian (孫堅), styledWentai (文台), was born in Fu Chun County, Wu Prefecture (Presently Fu Yang, Zhe Jiang Province). Father of Sun Ce, Sun Quan, and the kingdom of Wu. Sun Jian was affiliated with the Han Dynasty, Yuan Shu, and the Wu Kingdom. Sun Jian lived during the Later Han Dynasty periods of Chinese history.
Return to theThree Kingdoms Encyclopediaor browse theEncyclopedia Directory.

Fact vs Fiction
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
Sun Jian gave the Imperial Seal to Yuan Shu after finding it in a well. He did not hold the Seal for himself.
During the coalition against Dong Zhuo, Sun Jian served as a subordinate of Yuan Shu, not as himself.
Sun Jian did not withdraw from the anti-Dong Zhuo alliance, but rather was the only general to achieve success in the operations.
Sun Jian attacked Liu Biao on Yuan Shu’s orders, not because Liu Biao attacked first. In addition, during this time, Sun Jian was based in Henan, not Changsha as the novel states.
Sun Jian never killed the fictional Chen Sheng.
The ‘Magnificent Five of Wu’ is a fan creation with no historical basis. Sun Jian was not a part of any such group.

BiographyInternet (Trusted)
Sun Jian gained fame in his youth for a daring attack on a group of pirates and then for defeating the rebel Xu Chang. Made Zhu Jun’s Associate Major during the Yellow Turban rebellion, gathering a thousand men and matchless in deed, he was first over the walls at Wancheng.
Sun Jian fought against Bian Zhang in Liang under Zhang Wen and defeated Ou Xing and his allies, for which Wentai was enoffed as Marquis of Wucheng. Sun Jian raised troops against Dong Zhuo, executing Wang Rui and Zhang Zi along the way before meeting up with Yuan Shu. Sun Jian met with great success against the Imperial Army, killing Hua Xiong in the process and was the first into Luo Yang.
Sun Jian was sent against Liu Biao by Yuan Shu where he defeated Huang Zu but was soon killed by an arrow in uncertain circumstances.
